title: Puzzle Bookcase
summary: A tall 1x12 bookcase with staggered shelves and interlocking cubbies that clip on the side — a modular system you can grow and rearrange.
description:
The 'puzzle' in this bookcase is that it's modular: a tall side bookcase with staggered shelves, plus detachable cubbies that fasten to the side so you can extend and rearrange the storage like puzzle pieces. Build one side unit as your spine, then add cubbies as you need them.

Everything is 1x12 lumber with a plywood or MDF back on the cubbies, so it's simple casework — the interest is all in the layout. The staggered shelf spacing is set by measuring from the bottom of the bookcase to the bottom of each shelf (not shelf-to-shelf), so work off a single reference line down the side and the stagger comes out even. The back gets nailed on flush at the back outside corner and stiffens the whole unit.

A couple of things keep it honest. The bottom cubby side runs all the way to the floor to carry load, while the upper cubby sides are shorter — mind which is which. When you clip a cubby onto the side bookcase, use 1-1/4" screws through the shared side. And because it's tall and modular, it can get top-heavy: anchor it to the wall to prevent tipping, especially in a kid's room.
timeLabel: 5–8 hrs for a side unit plus a cubby

materials.name: 1x12 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Per side bookcase: shelves, side, back.

steps.title: Build the shelves and side
steps.body: Fasten the shelves to the side of the bookcase. The measurements are from the bottom of the bookcase to the bottom of each shelf, so mark a single reference line down the side and set the staggered spacing off it.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: Fasten the back to the back of the shelves and side, keeping the back outside corner flush with the back edge of the side. Nail through the back into the shelf and side edges. The back squares and stiffens the unit.

steps.title: Add cubby sides and footer
steps.body: Attach the cubby sides, outside edges flush — the bottom cubby side reaches all the way to the bottom of the bookcase to carry load, the upper ones are shorter. Then fasten the footer.

steps.title: Build a cubby
steps.body: Build a cubby from its top, bottom, sides, and MDF back, keeping outside edges flush. This is the module that clips onto the side bookcase.

steps.title: Assemble and anchor
steps.body: To add cubbies, fasten them to the side bookcases with 1-1/4" screws through the shared side. Because it's tall and modular, anchor the assembly to the wall to prevent tipping. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Tall bookcase with staggered shelves and interlocking cubbies
